title: An AI Company Wants to Build Next to Nashville's Zoo. The Leopards Said No.
script: Leopards vs. servers! DC Blox, an AI data center company, proposed a 69,000-square-foot facility next to the Nashville Zoo. The problem: the construction would sit about 50 yards from the zoo's critically endangered clouded leopards, which are notoriously sensitive to mechanical noise — disrupting the sound levels could end the zoo's breeding program. Over 300,000 people have signed a petition against the project. Country star Brad Paisley called it a nightmare scenario. Nashville Mayor Freddie O'Connell said the application was unusual and asked the city's legal team to investigate. The zoo says stormwater from the data center site already drains onto its grounds. Are clouded leopards really a match for artificial intelligence?
